opp.name: ALTRAD_S2P
opp.next_step: No update Feedback last session most impressive demo so far Pending RFP release in May - AG to connect with Diamond pending date Indicative pricing given c550k View: Early engagement places us well bur pricing is high. Receive RFI & confirmation of selection to next stage TOP 5 vendors based primarily on AI capabilities which is in our favour
opp.ais_why:
• Four Avoma meetings June 2025 to Feb 2026 with consistent Diamond Fianka, Lee Poulton and Natalie Davis engagement ‚Äî same stakeholders across 8+ months indicates relationship depth. Latest 1.8-hour demo on 18 Feb 2026 ‚Äî notes not retrievable but Merlin is in product scope alongside core S2P modules (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iRequest, iSave, iSource). No ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze.
• Formal Evaluation stage at $550K with BD_Engagement Engaged. Repeat 2-hour demo sessions with the same attendees indicate buyer commitment. Pricing meeting in June 2025 separately. The consistent multi-stakeholder cadence across 8 months at Formal Evaluation stage is strong commercial momentum but the AI dimension is thin.
• Medium confidence: deep engagement and Formal Evaluation stage support the score, but only one AI product (Merlin) in scope and the latest demo notes are inaccessible. Score retained at 6 ‚Äî defaulted DOWN at 6/7 due to no explicit AI POC or champion evidence captured in retrievable transcripts.

items.quote: we've got to get the Capex approval... our IT team will tie it all up
TOP 5 vendors based primarily on AI capabilities

metrics.narrative: The business problem is clear and quantified at operational scale: ALTRAD UK runs a large procurement operation on Business Central (implemented 2019, upgraded to BC in the last two years) that Natalie Davis described as 'slowing down, clunky, not user friendly' with about 500 users raising requisitions against roughly 20,000 catalog items (source: Natalie Davis, demo 2026-02-18). Pain is concrete and manual: duplicate POs from address formatting errors, no catalog images, table locks when finance runs reports, manual supplier onboarding via Nintex/SharePoint forms, manual Creditsafe checks, and spreadsheet-based supplier KPI tracking (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). No ROI, savings target, or payback threshold has ever been stated by the buyer, so the value envelope is unquantified; quantifying it against the Business Central pain remains the open move.

champion.narrative: Diamond Fianka has been the consistent point of contact and advocate across four touchpoints from June 2025 to Feb 2026: she initiated the engagement, shared requirements and module lists in advance, and stayed engaged through the Feb demo where buyer feedback was 'most impressive demo so far' (source: Avoma calls 2025-06-04 through 2026-02-18, Next_Step 2026-06-16). However her advocacy is developing, not strong: she lacks budget authority, no economic buyer or executive sponsor has been mapped behind her, and she has gone silent for 135 days with the promised RFP never issued. On the Feb call she did not attend personally in a driving role (Natalie Davis carried the discovery), which raises a question over her current ownership. Securing and re-activating a champion with power is the priority.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named by the buyer or logged in Salesforce. Diamond Fianka's process is an RFI shortlisting the 'top 5 vendors based primarily on AI capabilities' (source: Next_Step 2026-06-16), and Zycus was rated the most impressive demo so far, but the other four vendors have never been disclosed. Because the shortlist is AI-capability led, the likely peer set is the standard S2P field, but naming any specific rival would be fabrication on current evidence. The competitive picture is a genuine blind spot; discovering the shortlist by re-engaging Diamond is a ranked move, and until then the how-we-win is to lead hard with the Merlin agentic / intake-to-outcomes AI story that already resonated.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed, specific, and owner-attributed. Natalie Davis (Procurement Manager, UK) owns the day-to-day requisition-to-PO operation and articulated the core pain directly: a clunky Business Central ERP that duplicates POs, locks under finance reporting, and offers no catalog images across ~20,000 items, plus fully manual supplier onboarding, credit checks, and spreadsheet KPI tracking (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). Diamond Fianka owns the contracting and supplier-management side and confirmed the pain of chasing internal feedback for supplier reviews ('the pain he has trying to get feedback from people when he's doing his contract reviews') (source: Diamond Fianka / Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). Pain is operational-inefficiency driven; the business has already decided the current system 'is not where we want to be'.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are largely undiscussed and low-complexity on the buyer side. Natalie Davis described a lean legal function ('our legal team is limited... model form contracts we amend ourselves', approval from a commercial director) and confirmed no e-signature platform ('we print, we sign, we scan') (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). No MSA/SOW, InfoSec, or procurement-channel path has been scoped, and no contract-expiration forcing function exists (current stack is owned Business Central, not a renewing vendor). Visibility here will only come once the RFP is live and a preferred vendor is selected.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped or engaged. Natalie Davis described a decentralised, IT-coordinated buying effort ('all the different functions that utilize the ERP have been tasked with finding a solution... our IT team will tie it all up') with Capex approval and business cases still to be built over a 12 to 18 month horizon (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). Lee Poulton (Head of Procurement) is the only Salesforce contact role but has never joined a call. At a $550k spend the sign-off almost certainly sits at finance/director level, and none of that chain has been contacted. Identifying and reaching the budget owner is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a research-and-shortlist process ahead of a formal tender: each ERP-dependent function was tasked with finding a solution, IT will consolidate, business cases and Capex approval must be built, and the buyer put the realistic go-live at 12 to 18 months (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). The intended next gate is an RFP release (flagged for May 2026 in Next_Step) leading to selection of the top 5 vendors on AI capability. That RFP has not materialised, so the process has stalled at the pre-RFP research stage with no confirmed decision date or approval chain documented.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's criteria are AI-led and adoption-led. The Next_Step records selection to 'TOP 5 vendors based primarily on AI capabilities which is in our favour', and on the Feb demo Natalie Davis reacted strongly to Merlin intake ('I'm in shock, I've not seen anything like that before... it makes it really easy'), the 90%+ adoption claim, third-party risk integrations (EcoVadis, D&B, BvD), invoice/PO three-way match automation, and contract redline risk scoring (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). Usability and reducing manual effort are explicit criteria. The criteria have not yet been formalised in a written RFI/RFP, so the weighting is inferred from demo reactions rather than a scored requirement document.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS 6.0 / Moderate. Calls show genuine buyer appetite for the AI story (Natalie Davis reacted strongly to Merlin intake and agentic sourcing) but engagement is demo-only with no AI POC and the buyer is early in an AI-led selection. Field read is fair; calls suggest slightly under-rated on appetite but the thin single-AI-product scope justifies Moderate.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AIS 6.0 / Moderate.

bullets.full: No executive access yet — No economic buyer is mapped or engaged. Natalie Davis described a decentralised, IT-coordinated buying effort ('all the different functions that utilize the ERP have been tasked with finding a solution... our IT team will tie it all up') with Capex approval and business cases still to be built over a 12 to 18 month horizon (source: Natalie Davis, 2026-02-18). Lee Poulton (Head of Procurement) is the only Salesforce contact role but has never joined a call. At a $550k spend the sign-off almost certainly sits at finance/director level, and none of that chain has been contacted. Identifying and reaching the budget owner is a ranked move.
bullets.text: No executive access yet — No economic buyer is mapped or engaged.

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — The competitive field is unknown: no competitor has been named by the buyer or logged in Salesforce. Diamond Fianka is running an RFI to shortlist the 'top 5 vendors based primarily on AI capabilities' (Next_Step 2026-06-16), and Zycus feedback was 'most impressive demo so far', but the other four vendors were never disclosed. This is a total competitive blind spot, made worse by 135 days of silence during which a rival may have advanced or the RFI may have stalled. The single most important competitive action is not to rank an invented field but to re-engage Diamond and explicitly ask where we stand and who else is in the top 5.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — The competitive field is unknown: no competitor has been named by the buyer or logged in Salesforce.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Merlin agentic / intake-to-outcomes story landed strongly on the Feb demo (adoption, autonomous negotiation, Merlin risk radar, AI contract scoring all drew positive buyer reactions), and the RFP is being scored primarily on AI capability, which favours Zycus. But only one AI product (Merlin) is in scope, no AI POC has been run, and the narrative is not in front of a decision-maker.

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: The competitive field is unknown: no competitor has been named by the buyer or logged in Salesforce.

bullets.text: No economic buyer or executive sponsor mapped; single-thread to a champion without budget authority.
Buyer's own 12 to 18 month cycle with unapproved Capex conflicts with the 2026-11-30 close date; close date is a placeholder.
Buyer flagged the ~c550k indicative pricing as high; no value case built to justify it.
Business cases and Capex approval not yet started; RFP promised for May 2026 never released.

Reach Lee Poulton, Head of Procurement, to secure sponsorship and map the budget approver. (by 17 Jul)

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 26 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum is flat at 50: no forward or backward motion was observed.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 9 is anchored on the stage win (26), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate. Calls show genuine buyer appetite for the AI story (Natalie Davis reacted strongly to Merlin intake and agentic sourcing) but engagement is demo-only with no AI POC and the buyer is early in an AI-led selection. Field read is fair; calls suggest slightly under-rated on appetite but the thin single-AI-product scope justifies Moderate.

items: Positive Feb 2026 demo ('most impressive demo so far') not converted to a written next step.
RFP promised for May 2026 never issued; no buyer reply in 135 days.
Owner reassigned mid-cycle (Rozalyn Orme to Claire Hudson, 2026-05-13), a continuity risk on an already-quiet deal.
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled to dark: strong positive signal in Feb 2026 followed by 135 days of no buyer contact and a missed RFP milestone.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-11-30 (150 days out). Created 2025-06-04, in Formal Evaluation for ~130 days (stage change 2026-02-23), no Formal_Eval_Submission_Date recorded. Last buyer engagement 2026-02-18 demo; last Salesforce activity 2026-02-19 (135 days ago). Next_Step (2026-06-16) still reads 'Pending RFP release in May' but May 2026 has passed with no RFP and no recorded contact. Cold 60+ days at an early stage forces Off Track; the Nov close date is a placeholder.
evidence: Last verified Salesforce activity 2026-02-19, 135 days ago (source: LastActivityDate).
Last buyer call: 2026-02-18 demo with Diamond Fianka and Natalie Davis; buyer feedback 'most impressive demo so far' (source: Avoma demo 2026-02-18, Next_Step 2026-06-16).
Next_Step (2026-06-16) states 'Pending RFP release in May - AG to connect with Diamond pending date'; May 2026 passed with no RFP issued and no recorded contact (source: Next_Step__c).
No completed tasks or buyer-facing activity logged since February 2026 (source: Salesforce activity history, empty last 120d).
Owner reassigned to Claire Hudson on 2026-05-13 and close date pushed 2026-06-30 to 2026-11-30 on 2026-05-12 (source: OpportunityFieldHistory); both are internal moves, not buyer engagement.
Indicative pricing ~c550k given June 2025; buyer flagged pricing as high and no budget sign-off followed (source: Next_Step__c view, pricing meeting 2025-06-25).
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has gone cold: no buyer contact since the 18 Feb 2026 demo (135 days). The RFP promised for May 2026 never issued and no follow-up has landed at Formal Evaluation stage.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (retain, but at risk of Omitted if no re-engagement in 30 days)

flags: Re-open the dormant relationship: owner Claire Hudson should reach Diamond Fianka directly for RFP timing and where Zycus stands after 135 days silent.
Multi-thread beyond Diamond: engage Lee Poulton (Head of Procurement, the sole contact role) and identify the finance/Capex approver for a $550k spend.
Close the competitive blind spot: ask Diamond who the other four AI-capability shortlist vendors are and pre-position the Merlin agentic advantage.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cold at an early stage with a single-thread to a champion who lacks authority. Win-strategy priority is to re-open the relationship, multi-thread to power, and reclaim visibility into the RFP and competitive shortlist.

competitive_position.summary: The competitive field is unknown: no competitor has been named by the buyer or logged in Salesforce. Diamond Fianka is running an RFI to shortlist the 'top 5 vendors based primarily on AI capabilities' (Next_Step 2026-06-16), and Zycus feedback was 'most impressive demo so far', but the other four vendors were never disclosed. This is a total competitive blind spot, made worse by 135 days of silence during which a rival may have advanced or the RFI may have stalled. The single most important competitive action is not to rank an invented field but to re-engage Diamond and explicitly ask where we stand and who else is in the top 5.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus mapped strongly to the buyer's stated needs on the Feb demo: unified AI intake, high adoption, third-party risk integrations, automated three-way match, and AI contract redline scoring all landed positively with Natalie Davis. The main exposure is commercial (pricing flagged high) and the long, unfunded 12 to 18 month buyer timeline.
